title: The Music Box
summary: A narrator in Vienna describes a small wooden music box she received as a child and the simple song it plays.
body: I have a small wooden music box on the shelf. It is brown and gold. The lid has a tiny painted bird on top. The box came from a small shop in old Vienna. My aunt gave it to me on my seventh birthday. She said, 'This box is small, but it has a sweet song.' I opened it for the first time and a slow tune played. It is only twelve notes long. The same twelve notes, again and again. To play the music, you turn a little silver key on the back. When the key is full, the music plays for one minute. Then the box is quiet. When I was small, I played it every night before bed. I learned the notes by heart. I sang along. My cat liked it too. He sat by the box with his eyes closed. My aunt is very old now. She lives in a small house outside the city. I take the music box to her on Sunday. She closes her eyes when it plays. She smiles. The song is short, but our memory is long.

annotations.exampleEn: The music box plays a slow tune.
She has a small wooden box.
Lift the lid of the box gently.
The song has only twelve notes.
She read the book again and again.
He used a small silver key.
I know the song by heart.

annotations.meaningVi: hát theo (quá khứ của sing along)
annotations.exampleEn: We sang along with the radio.
He listened with his eyes closed.

grammarNotes.title: When + past simple to talk about routines in the past
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'When I was small, I played …' kể về thói quen lúc nhỏ. Dùng quá khứ đơn trong cả hai vế: 'When I was small, I played it every night before bed.' / 'He sat by the box with his eyes closed.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'When I was small, I played …' tells about a past habit. Use simple past in both halves: 'When I was small, I played it every night before bed.' / 'He sat by the box with his eyes closed.'

grammarNotes.title: Adjective + noun order
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Tính từ luôn đứng trước danh từ: 'a small wooden music box', 'a tiny painted bird', 'a slow tune', 'a little silver key'. Nếu có nhiều tính từ, thứ tự thường là: ý kiến → kích thước → tuổi → màu sắc → chất liệu.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Adjectives go before the noun: 'a small wooden music box', 'a tiny painted bird', 'a slow tune', 'a little silver key'. With several adjectives, the usual order is: opinion → size → age → colour → material.

translationVi: Trên kệ nhà tôi có một cái hộp nhạc nhỏ bằng gỗ. Nó màu nâu và vàng. Nắp hộp có hình một con chim nhỏ được vẽ ở trên. Cái hộp đến từ một cửa hàng nhỏ ở khu phố cổ Vienna. Dì tôi tặng tôi vào sinh nhật bảy tuổi. Dì nói: 'Cái hộp nhỏ thôi nhưng nó có một bài hát ngọt ngào.' Tôi mở nó lần đầu tiên và một giai điệu chậm rãi vang lên. Bài hát chỉ dài mười hai nốt. Cùng mười hai nốt đó, lặp đi lặp lại. Để chơi nhạc, bạn vặn cái chìa khoá bạc nhỏ ở phía sau. Khi chìa đã lên cót đầy, nhạc sẽ chơi trong khoảng một phút. Sau đó hộp im lặng. Hồi nhỏ tôi mở nó mỗi tối trước khi đi ngủ. Tôi thuộc các nốt nhạc nằm lòng. Tôi hát theo. Con mèo của tôi cũng thích. Nó ngồi bên cái hộp, mắt nhắm. Dì tôi giờ đã già lắm. Dì sống trong một căn nhà nhỏ ngoài thành phố. Mỗi chủ nhật tôi mang cái hộp nhạc đến cho dì. Dì nhắm mắt khi nhạc chơi. Dì cười. Bài hát thì ngắn, nhưng kỉ niệm của hai dì cháu thì dài.

questions.prompt: Where does the narrator keep the music box?
questions.explanation: 'I have a small wooden music box on the shelf.'

questions.prompt: What is painted on the lid?
questions.explanation: 'The lid has a tiny painted bird on top.'

questions.prompt: Who gave her the music box?
questions.explanation: 'My aunt gave it to me on my seventh birthday.'

questions.prompt: How old was the narrator when she got it?
How many notes does the song have?
How do you start the music?
questions.explanation: 'you turn a little silver key on the back.'

questions.prompt: How long does the music play?
When did she play it as a child?
questions.explanation: 'I played it every night before bed.'

options: He sat by the box with his eyes closed
questions.explanation: 'He sat by the box with his eyes closed.'

questions.prompt: Where does the aunt live now?
options: In a flat in old Vienna
In a small house outside the city

questions.prompt: When does the narrator visit her aunt with the music box?
questions.explanation: 'I take the music box to her on Sunday.'

questions.prompt: What is the meaning of the last sentence?
options: The memory is bigger than the short tune
questions.explanation: 'The song is short, but our memory is long.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Some objects connect us to a person we love. Why does music bring memories back so strongly?
